ab: na paljilji ta ku ama aken ati kama ta tiyiv.
ch: 昨天我和爸爸去拜訪我的叔叔。
en: Yesterday, my father and I visited my uncle.

ab: na mapasaqetju a kula ni kama i kuliw.
tjumaljan amen ni madju na, makapalak tu ita berung a qaljiw na umaq.
ch: 他告訴我們，房子的屋頂破了一個洞。
en: He told us that there was a hole in the roof of his house.

ab: ka ljemenguaq ta qaljiw na umaq timadju, matjani anga kemasi ta qaljiw na umaq.
ch: 他在修理屋頂時，從屋頂摔下來了。
en: He fell off the roof while repairing it.

ab: na kemacu ti kama ta na gung a vudjulj ‘ata saviki sipavay djay ama.
ch: 爸爸帶了牛肉和檳榔給叔叔。
en: My father brought beef and betel nuts for my uncle.

ab: i ’umaan a mazazepezep a ken ati ama.liyav a milimilinga sini djawcikel ni madju.
ch: 我和叔叔在房間裡聊天，他分享了很多故事。
en: My uncle and I chatted in the room, and he shared many stories.

ab: a muri vayika anga ken, zemengel ti ama tu mekacanga ken, mapavalit tu djasawqaljay tu.
ch: 我要離開前，叔叔誇我長高了，也變得更帥氣了。
en: Before I left, my uncle praised me for growing taller and becoming more handsome.

ab: ’evu ken, “nu menguwa anga su kula ama, keljuanga ta nia umaq kivangavang nu djayvililj tu.”
ch: 我說：「等叔叔腳好了，下次也來我們家玩。」
en: I said, “When your foot gets better, come to our house next time and visit us.”
